libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No environment variables or system properties are required. Tests should initialize and exercise the provider directly, e.g. Conscrypt.checkAvailability(), Conscrypt.newProvider(), SSLContext with the Conscrypt provider, key/cipher/signature APIs, and local loopback sockets if socket coverage is needed.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8879 [Automation] native-image run fails for org.conscrypt:conscrypt-openjdk-uber:1.4.1; label=fails-native-image-run; body_chars=8015

risks: Conscrypt extracts and loads a native library from the jar; environments with a noexec temp directory may need -Dorg.conscrypt.native.workdir pointing at an executable directory, but this is not required for normal Ubuntu CI.

Some deeper platform/socket paths may still require the existing Java/native-image --add-opens options already present in the current test project; this is Gradle test configuration, not an extra dependency or Docker setup.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No concrete extra setup is needed: org.conscrypt:conscrypt-openjdk-uber:1.4.1 has no Maven dependencies, the uber artifact embeds the platform native libraries, and normal meaningful use is local JCA/JSSE provider initialization via Conscrypt without Docker or external services.
